title: Little Red Riding Hood
content:
Once upon a time there was a dear little girl who was loved by everyone who looked at her. Her grandmother gave her a little red riding hood, and she wore it so often that everyone called her Little Red Riding Hood.

One day her mother said to her, "Come, Little Red Riding Hood, here is a cake and a bottle of milk for your grandmother. She is ill and weak, and this will do her good. Go now, and do not stray from the path."

Little Red Riding Hood set out at once. On the way, she met a wolf.

"Good day, Little Red Riding Hood," said the wolf. "Where are you going so early?"

"To my grandmother's house," she replied.

"And where does your grandmother live?" asked the wolf.

"Her house stands under three large oak trees," said Little Red Riding Hood.

The wolf thought to himself, "This tender young thing would be a delicious morsel."

The wolf ran ahead and knocked at the grandmother's door.

"Who is there?" called the grandmother.

"Little Red Riding Hood," replied the wolf, making his voice soft.

The wolf went inside and swallowed the grandmother whole. Then he put on her nightgown and cap and got into her bed.

When Little Red Riding Hood arrived, she noticed something strange about her grandmother.

"What big ears you have, Grandmother!"

"All the better to hear you with, my dear."

"What big eyes you have!"

"All the better to see you with."

"What big teeth you have!"

"All the better to eat you with!" And the wolf leaped out of bed.

Just then, a huntsman was passing by. He heard the commotion and burst in. He chased the wolf away!

The grandmother was saved, and Little Red Riding Hood promised never to stray from the path again.

The End.

author: Brothers Grimm
